coverage.note: Qualitative: every changed .cs (data/this.cs, Wire.cs, Navigation.cs, list/Conversion.cs, GoalCall.cs, variable/set.cs, list/add.cs, MathPolicy.cs, OpenAi.cs) is exercised by a named, strong-assertion test verified during this audit. Instrumented coverage.json deprioritized in favor of per-anchor assertion-strength review.

findings.issue: ead0caa83 changed BOTH variable.set and list.add to ShallowClone (replacing a JSON deep-clone that dropped Signatures / materialized lazy values). list.add got a real regression test (SignedDataSurvivesInList.test.goal: sign -> add to list -> verify element). The symmetric variable.set List/Dict arm has NO equivalent: Set_ListValue/DictValue_StoresDistinctListInstance only assert the OUTER container is a distinct instance, with plain string elements -- a nested signed/lazy Data is never put through that arm.
findings.impact: If a future change to the param-resolution container walk or ShallowClone re-wrapped nested elements, a signed Data bound inside a list-literal via variable.set could silently lose its Signature with no test catching it. This is exactly the class of bug (signed Data through a collection) that 'hid for so long' per the coder's own v3 report.
findings.suggestion: Add a goal regression mirroring SignedDataSurvivesInList but via set: `sign -> set %bundle% = [%signed%] -> verify %bundle[0]%`, OR a C# test Set_ListWithNestedSignedData_PreservesElementSignature. NOTE: tester probe-confirmed the CURRENT behavior is CORRECT (signature survives + verifies), so this is a regression-pinning gap, not a live bug.

findings.issue: The goal tamper test mutates via string interpolation (`set %tampered% = "%signed% (tampered)"`), which produces a fresh plain-text Data with no Signature. So verify likely fails with a no-signature / missing-signature path rather than a hash-MISMATCH path -- the test passes for a possibly-weaker reason than its name implies.
findings.impact: On its own this would not distinguish 'tamper detected' from 'unsigned data rejected'.
findings.suggestion: Acceptable AS-IS: the goal comment explicitly defers the strict raw-tamper path to C#, and that anchor is real and strong -- FailureMatrixTests.SigningVerify_AfterWireByteTamper_ReturnsDataHashMismatch asserts Error.Key == 'DataHashMismatch'. No action required; documented for transparency.

findings.issue: No baseline-tests.md exists for any coder version (v1/v2/v3). Tester workflow step 2 requires it to separate regressions from pre-existing failures.
findings.impact: Low this round -- both suites are fully green (273/0, 4021/0), so there are no failures to attribute. Would matter if anything were red.
findings.suggestion: Coder should drop baseline-tests.md before starting future versions.

summary: lazy-deserialize v3 is honestly green. Clean rebuild gives 273/273 plang (deterministic across 2 runs, no .pr rewritten) and 4021/4021 C#. Every LazyDeserialize .pr matches its step text (no builder false-greens). The goal-layer 'smoke' tests legitimately defer strict assertions to C#, and all 9 claimed C# anchors were audited and found to exist with strong assertions (DataHashMismatch error key, MaterializeCount==0, 'add'/'as' substrings, PKind.Long + 5000000000, name-excluded canonical hash, etc.). The one genuine gap -- variable.set's List/Dict arm (symmetric half of the ead0caa83 ShallowClone fix) lacks a nested-signed-Data regression -- was probed empirically and the current behavior is CORRECT, so it is a regression-pinning gap (minor), not a live false-green. No critical or major findings. PASS.
